About Us

Amerus Insurance Group is a comprehensive insurance and financial planning solutions provider that serves employers, individuals, families, and seniors across the United States. Originally founded as the Brotherhood of American Yeomen in 1896, the company has over a century of experience in providing life insurance, annuities, and retirement planning services. While acquired by Aviva plc in 2006, the Amerus brand continues to operate as a trusted insurance broker and advisor, offering personalized coverage solutions through licensed professionals. The company specializes in coordinating comprehensive insurance portfolios including group benefits, commercial insurance, personal policies, and Medicare planning, with a unique year-round enrollment capability that sets them apart from traditional insurance providers.

We work directly with businesses and individuals to understand their specific needs and build solutions that fit.

Our team brings hands-on experience and a practical approach to every project we take on.

Dental Insurance for Families: by Amerus Insurance Group

Dental Insurance for Families: Protecting Your Loved Ones' Oral Health with Amerus Insurance Group

A single dental emergency can cost a family thousands of dollars, turning routine oral care from a health priority into a financial burden that many families simply cannot afford. When children need braces, parents require root canals, or unexpected dental issues arise, the expenses quickly compound—leaving many families to choose between proper oral healthcare and other essential needs.

Dental insurance for families provides coverage for a range of oral healthcare services, helping to reduce out-of-pocket costs and ensure regular preventative care for every family member. Amerus Insurance Group offers various plans tailored to meet different family needs and budgets, prioritizing your family’s overall well-being through comprehensive dental coverage solutions.

The statistics surrounding family dental health reveal a concerning reality. According to the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention, tooth decay remains one of the most common chronic diseases among children, yet many families delay or skip necessary treatments due to cost concerns. This cycle of deferred care often leads to more complex and expensive procedures down the road, making affordable dental plans for families not just a convenience, but a crucial investment in long-term health and financial stability.

Quality family dental coverage options serve as a protective barrier against these escalating costs while encouraging proactive oral healthcare habits. When families have access to regular cleanings, preventative treatments, and early intervention services, they can address minor issues before they develop into major problems requiring extensive procedures.

“Preventative dental care covered by insurance saves families an average of 40% on long-term oral health expenses while ensuring children develop healthy habits that last a lifetime.”

The dental insurance for families experts at Amerus Insurance Group understand that every family’s needs are unique. Through their client-centered approach and personalized solutions, families can access comprehensive coverage that includes everything from routine cleanings and fluoride treatments to orthodontics and emergency care. Their experienced professionals provide trusted guidance throughout the selection process, helping families compare options and identify the best dental insurance for kids and adults alike.

Modern dental insurance has evolved beyond basic coverage to include innovative features like telehealth consultations, expanded network options, and flexible payment structures that make quality oral healthcare accessible to families across different income levels and geographic locations.

What is a Family Dental Plan and How Does it Benefit You?

A family dental plan is a single insurance policy that covers multiple members of a household, typically providing preventative, basic, and major restorative services at a reduced cost. It simplifies dental care management and makes essential treatments more accessible for everyone under one umbrella policy, offering families a streamlined approach to maintaining optimal oral health.

Unlike individual dental policies, family dental coverage options are designed to accommodate the varying needs of different family members while providing substantial cost savings through group coverage. These comprehensive plans typically include coverage for routine cleanings, fluoride treatments, sealants, fillings, crowns, and emergency dental care across all age groups.

Key Components of Family Dental Coverage

Modern family dental insurance plans include several essential components that address the diverse oral health needs of household members:

  • Preventative care coverage at 100% including regular cleanings, exams, and X-rays

  • Basic restorative services such as fillings and simple extractions at 70-80% coverage

  • Major restorative procedures including crowns, bridges, and root canals at 50% coverage

  • Pediatric dental benefits with enhanced coverage for children’s specialized treatments

  • Orthodontic coverage for both children and adults, typically with separate annual maximums

The best dental insurance for kids often includes additional preventative measures like fluoride treatments and sealants, recognizing that early intervention significantly reduces long-term dental costs.

“Family dental plans provide the financial foundation for proactive oral healthcare, allowing families to address dental issues before they become costly emergencies,” notes dental insurance specialists.

Long-Term Financial Advantages

The financial benefits of affordable dental plans for families extend far beyond immediate savings. Families typically save 30-50% on annual dental expenses compared to paying out-of-pocket for individual treatments. With routine cleanings costing $100-200 per visit and major procedures like crowns ranging from $800-1,500, comprehensive family coverage quickly demonstrates its value.

Group dental insurance for families through employer-sponsored plans often provides the most cost-effective solution, with employers frequently subsidizing a significant portion of premium costs. When evaluating options, families should consider annual maximums, waiting periods for major services, and network provider accessibility.

The Dental Insurance for Families experts at Amerus Insurance Group emphasize that selecting the right family dental plan requires careful consideration of each family member’s specific needs, existing dental health status, and anticipated treatment requirements to maximize both coverage benefits and long-term savings.

Exploring Types of Family Dental Insurance Plans

Family dental insurance plans typically fall into categories like PPO, HMO, or discount plans, each offering different levels of flexibility regarding dentist choice and payment structures. Understanding these options is essential for making informed decisions about your family’s oral health coverage.

PPO Plans: Flexibility and Choice for Your Family

Preferred Provider Organization (PPO) plans remain the most popular choice among families seeking affordable dental plans for families. These plans offer the greatest flexibility in choosing dental providers while maintaining cost-effective coverage. With PPO plans, families can visit any licensed dentist, though staying within the network provides maximum savings through negotiated rates.

Key advantages of PPO plans include:

  • Freedom to choose any dentist without referrals

  • Higher reimbursement rates for in-network providers (typically 80-100% for preventive care)

  • No requirement for primary care dentist designation

  • Coverage for specialist visits without prior authorization

HMO Plans: Structured Care and Cost Savings

Health Maintenance Organization (HMO) dental plans provide structured care through a network of designated providers, making them an excellent option for best dental insurance for kids when consistent, coordinated care is prioritized. These plans typically feature lower monthly premiums and predictable copayments.

HMO dental plans typically offer:

  • Lower monthly premiums compared to PPO options

  • Predictable copayments for services

  • Emphasis on preventive care with minimal out-of-pocket costs

  • Requirement to select a primary care dentist

Discount Dental Plans for Families: An Alternative Approach

Discount dental plans represent an alternative to traditional insurance, offering immediate savings on dental services through participating provider networks. These plans work particularly well for families seeking low cost family dental insurance alternatives without waiting periods or annual maximums.

Discount dental plans can provide 10-60% savings on dental procedures, making them an attractive option for families with immediate dental needs or those seeking supplemental coverage.

Group Dental Insurance for Families: Employer-Sponsored Options

Group dental insurance for families through employer-sponsored programs often provides the most comprehensive and cost-effective coverage available. The dental insurance for families experts at Amerus Insurance Group work with employers to design benefit packages that meet diverse family needs while maintaining competitive premiums through group purchasing power.

Essential factors when evaluating family dental plans include:

  1. Annual maximum benefits (typically ranging from $1,000-$2,500 per person)

  2. Deductible requirements for basic and major services

  3. Pediatric dental coverage compliance with essential health benefits

  4. Orthodontic benefits for children and adults

  5. Network provider availability in your geographic area

When selecting coverage, consider your family’s specific needs, including the cost of family dental care and anticipated treatment requirements. Amerus Insurance Group’s proven group benefits administration strategies help families navigate these options with personalized solutions tailored to their unique circumstances, ensuring optimal coverage while maintaining affordability through trusted guidance from experienced professionals.

Why is Family Dental Coverage Essential for Your Household?

Investing in family dental coverage is essential to safeguard against unforeseen dental emergencies, encourage regular preventative care, and manage the overall cost of family dental care, especially for procedures like orthodontics or crowns. With dental treatment costs continuing to rise significantly, comprehensive coverage protects families from unexpected financial burdens while ensuring everyone receives necessary oral healthcare.

The Importance of Preventative Dental Care for Families

Preventative dental care serves as the foundation of long-term oral health, yet many families delay or skip routine treatments due to cost concerns. Regular cleanings, fluoride treatments, and early intervention can prevent minor issues from developing into expensive procedures requiring crowns, root canals, or extractions. The Dental Insurance for Families experts at Amerus Insurance Group emphasize that families with coverage are significantly more likely to maintain consistent preventative care schedules.

Modern family dental plans typically cover 100% of preventative services, making routine care accessible and eliminating cost barriers that often prevent families from seeking timely treatment.

Managing the Cost of Family Dental Care: Beyond Routine Check-ups

While preventative care forms the baseline, families must prepare for more substantial dental investments. Restorative procedures like fillings, crowns, and bridges can cost thousands without insurance protection. Compare family dental plans to understand coverage percentages for major procedures, which typically range from 50-80% after deductibles.

Key financial benefits of family dental coverage include:

  1. Predictable annual costs through monthly premiums versus surprise expenses

  2. Negotiated provider rates that reduce out-of-pocket expenses significantly

  3. Annual maximum benefits that protect against catastrophic dental costs

  4. No waiting periods for preventative care with most group plans

Addressing Specific Needs: Pediatric Dental Coverage Plans and Orthodontics

Pediatric dental coverage plans address children’s unique oral health requirements, including space maintainers, sealants, and early orthodontic intervention. The best dental insurance for kids typically includes specialized coverage for treatments rarely needed by adults.

Orthodontic benefits deserve special consideration, as traditional braces or clear aligners represent substantial family investments. Quality coverage may provide 50% orthodontic benefits up to lifetime maximums of $1,000-$2,500 per child.

Benefits of Maintaining Continuous Oral Health Across All Ages

Amerus Insurance Group’s proven group benefits administration strategies recognize that continuous dental coverage prevents treatment gaps that lead to deteriorating oral health. Adults require different care focus areas, including periodontal treatment and restorative work, while seniors may need dentures or implant coverage.

Age-specific coverage priorities:

  • Children (0-12): Preventative care, sealants, early intervention

  • Teens (13-18): Orthodontics, wisdom tooth management, sports-related protection

  • Adults (19-64): Restorative procedures, periodontal care, cosmetic options

  • Seniors (65+): Denture coverage, implant support, specialized geriatric care

Comprehensive family coverage ensures every household member receives appropriate care without financial stress, supporting overall health outcomes and family wellbeing.

How to Choose the Best Dental Insurance for Kids and Your Whole Family

To choose the best family dental insurance, consider your family’s specific dental needs, budget, preferred dentists, and the extent of coverage for common procedures, including pediatric services and orthodontics. With dental insurance for families becoming increasingly essential, selecting the right plan requires careful evaluation of multiple factors that impact both coverage quality and long-term costs.

Essential Steps for Selecting Family Dental Coverage

  1. Assess your family’s current and anticipated dental needs

  2. Evaluate network restrictions and preferred provider options

  3. Compare deductibles, coinsurance rates, and annual maximums

  4. Review pediatric coverage requirements and orthodontic benefits

  5. Calculate total annual costs including premiums and out-of-pocket expenses

Assessing Your Family’s Unique Dental Needs and Priorities

Start by documenting each family member’s dental history and anticipated needs. Children typically require preventive care, fluoride treatments, and potential orthodontic work, while adults may need more extensive restorative procedures. The affordable dental plans for families experts at Amerus Insurance Group recommend creating a comprehensive needs assessment that includes:

  • Current dental health status for each family member

  • Anticipated procedures within the next 12-24 months

  • Preferred dental providers and their network participation

  • Budget constraints and premium tolerance levels

Modern families spend an average of $1,200-$2,400 annually on dental care, making comprehensive coverage evaluation crucial for financial planning.

Understanding Coverage: Deductibles, Coinsurance, and Annual Maximums

Deductibles represent your out-of-pocket expense before insurance coverage begins, typically ranging from $50-$150 per person or $150-$300 per family. Coinsurance determines your percentage of costs after meeting deductibles, with most plans covering 100% of preventive care, 80% of basic procedures, and 50% of major treatments. Annual maximums cap insurer payments, usually between $1,000-$2,000 per person annually.

How to Compare Family Dental Plans Effectively

When evaluating family dental coverage options, examine the total cost of ownership rather than just monthly premiums. Factor in deductibles, coinsurance percentages, annual maximums, and network restrictions. Group dental insurance typically offers better rates and broader coverage than individual plans, making employer-sponsored options particularly attractive.

The Amerus Insurance Group’s proven group benefits administration strategies emphasize comprehensive plan comparison tools that help families identify optimal coverage balances. Key comparison metrics include network size, waiting periods for major procedures, orthodontic coverage limits, and pediatric dental coverage plans that meet federal essential health benefit requirements.

Effective plan comparison requires analyzing real-world scenarios based on your family’s anticipated usage patterns, ensuring selected coverage aligns with both immediate needs and long-term dental health goals.

Frequently Asked Questions About Dental Insurance for Your Family

What age should children start dental coverage? Most dental insurance for families plans recommend coverage from birth, as pediatric dental benefits are essential health benefits under current healthcare regulations. Early coverage ensures routine cleanings, fluoride treatments, and preventive care that establish healthy habits for life.

How much does family dental insurance typically cost? Affordable dental plans for families through group benefits administration typically range from $30-80 monthly for comprehensive coverage. The exact cost depends on your chosen plan type, deductible levels, and whether you select in-network or out-of-network provider options.

What’s the difference between dental HMO and PPO plans for families? Dental HMOs require selecting a primary dentist and referrals for specialists, offering lower premiums but less flexibility. PPO plans provide broader provider networks and no referral requirements, making them popular choices when comparing family dental plans for busy households with varying scheduling needs.

“Group dental insurance for families through employer benefits typically offers 30-50% cost savings compared to individual market plans, making comprehensive coverage accessible for working families.” - Healthcare Economics Research

Are orthodontics covered under family dental plans? Most comprehensive plans include orthodontic coverage for children under 18, though adult orthodontics may require additional riders. Pediatric dental coverage plans mandated by federal guidelines ensure essential orthodontic treatment is included in qualifying health plans.

How do annual maximums work for family coverage? Family plans typically operate with per-person annual maximums ranging from $1,000-2,500, meaning each covered family member has their own benefit limit. This structure ensures one family member’s major treatment doesn’t exhaust benefits for other family members.

What preventive services are covered at 100%? Standard preventive care covered without cost-sharing includes:

  • Routine cleanings (typically twice yearly)

  • Oral examinations and X-rays

  • Fluoride treatments for children

  • Sealants for permanent molars

When can I enroll in family dental coverage? Unlike medical insurance, many group plans offer year-round enrollment capabilities. Amerus Insurance Group’s proven group benefits administration strategies include flexible enrollment periods that accommodate changing family needs without waiting for annual open enrollment windows.

How do I choose the best plan for my family’s needs? Consider your family’s current oral health status, preferred dentists, budget constraints, and anticipated treatment needs. Dental Insurance for Families experts at Amerus Insurance Group provide personalized guidance through comprehensive plan comparison tools, ensuring your selected coverage aligns with both immediate needs and long-term oral health goals.

Frequently Asked Questions

Here are the FAQ answers for Amerus Insurance Group:

Q: What is the average cost of family dental insurance?

The average cost of family dental insurance can vary widely based on factors such as plan type, location, and the number of family members, but generally ranges from $50 to $150 per month in 2026. At Amerus Insurance Group, we help clients find affordable dental plans for families that fit their budget without compromising on quality coverage. The specific cost will depend on the comprehensiveness of the plan and the benefits included, such as coverage for orthodontics or major procedures. Explore Dental Insurance for Families solutions tailored to your needs.

Q: What does family dental insurance typically cover?

Family dental insurance typically covers preventative care such as cleanings, exams, and routine X-rays, basic procedures like fillings and simple extractions, and often a percentage of major procedures like crowns, bridges, and root canals. Most plans prioritize preventive care, covering 80-100% of these services to encourage regular dental visits. The level of coverage for basic and major procedures will vary between different family dental coverage options. Amerus Insurance Group ensures you understand all aspects of “what is a family dental plan” before you choose.

Q: Is orthodontics covered by family dental insurance?

Coverage for orthodontics varies greatly by plan. Some premium family dental insurance plans include orthodontic benefits, especially for pediatric needs, while others may offer limited or no coverage. When looking for the best dental insurance for kids, it’s crucial to check for orthodontic benefits if this is a concern for your family. Many plans that do cover orthodontics may have specific age limits or waiting periods. Let Amerus Insurance Group’s expertise guide you through plans that offer robust orthodontic coverage.

Q: How do I choose the best dental plan for my family?

To choose the best dental plan for your family, assess their specific oral health needs, consider your budget, compare provider networks, and review annual maximums and deductibles. Think about the potential cost of family dental care for services like braces or extensive restorative work. Amerus Insurance Group simplifies the process of “how to choose family dental insurance” by providing clear comparisons and expert advice on various family dental coverage options to ensure you get the most value. Visit > to compare different plans.

Q: Are there waiting periods for family dental insurance?

Yes, many family dental insurance plans have waiting periods for certain procedures, especially major services, which can range from a few months to a year. For routine preventive care, waiting periods are often waived. It’s essential to understand these terms when you “compare family dental plans” to avoid unexpected out-of-pocket costs. Amerus Insurance Group helps clients find plans with favorable waiting period conditions, ensuring your family can access necessary care without undue delay. Learn more about Dental Insurance for Families solutions.

Q: What is the difference between an individual and family dental plan?

An individual dental plan covers only one person, while a family dental plan covers multiple members of the same household under a single policy, often at a more cost-effective rate per person. Family plans are ideal for households with two or more individuals, consolidating billing and simplifying administration. Opting for a family dental insurance plan from Amerus Insurance Group can be a smart financial decision, as it typically offers better value than purchasing separate individual policies for each family member for comprehensive group dental insurance for families. dental insurance for families benefits of family dental insurance how dental insurance works for families
